narrative.summary: You're on a scruffy stretch of Queen West where low lighting and close-set tables make it feel like you've stumbled into someone's very good dinner party—the kind where everyone's drinking natural wine and nobody's checking their phone. The Euro small-plates cooking actually understands restraint—creamy burrata, properly smoked trout whipped into a dip you'll want more bread for, duck crostini that people photograph and then immediately eat. Plan for $60-80 a person if you're sharing three or four plates and drinking wine that isn't on every other list in the city.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on a scruffy stretch of Queen West where the rent hasn't totally squeezed out the interesting spots yet. Inside, low lighting and close-set tables make it feel like you've stumbled into someone's very good dinner party—the kind where everyone's drinking natural wine and nobody's checking their phone. It's small enough that you can hear the kitchen working behind you.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is Euro small-plates cooking that actually understands restraint—creamy burrata, properly smoked trout whipped into a dip you'll want more bread for, duck crostini that people photograph and then immediately eat. The mortadella sandwich is the kind of simple-sounding thing that makes you realize the kitchen knows what it's doing. You'll spend $60-80 a person if you're sharing three or four plates and drinking wine that isn't on every other list in the city.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Bar Piquette is a great cozy spot for small plates and good wine. Although I didn’t get a photo of it their duck crostinis are ABSOLUTELY DIVINE. Smoked trout dip was delicious! The ambience is perfect for a whimsical or romantic night out if you want to start light with drinks and apps. Definitely recommend."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Had a date night at Bar Piquette, The atmosphere was cozy and perfect for a relaxed evening over great food and wine. We shared the squash, duck crostini, burrata, and the mortadella sandwich every dish was beautifully balanced and full of flavor, although my favorite was the duck and the mortadella.

We also picked a lovely bottle of wine that paired perfectly with everything. Highly recommend for anyone looking for an intimate spot with top-notch small plates and a great vibe."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Great vibes for fall. $12 on selected wines and good shareable portions. The duck crosonti was a perfect sweet and salty mix. The burrata could’ve had more to it. Mushroom duxelle with the garlic aoili is a great combination. Love the seasonal menu!"
